UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's basic profile information within the ExamMentor AI application. Assumes external authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

Exam.title: Exam
Exam.description: Defines a specific competitive exam supported by the platform (e.g., NEET, JEE, GATE).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Exam entity.

name.description: The common name or abbreviation of the exam (e.g., 'NEET', 'JEE Main').

fullForm.description: The full name of the exam (e.g., 'National Eligibility cum Entrance Test').

description.description: A brief description of the exam.

Subject.title: Subject
Subject.description: Represents an academic subject within a particular exam (e.g., Physics, Organic Chemistry).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subject entity.

examId.description: Reference to the Exam this subject belongs to. (Relationship: Exam 1:N Subject)

description.description: A brief description of the subject.

Chapter.title: Chapter
Chapter.description: Represents a specific chapter or topic within a subject.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Chapter entity.

subjectId.description: Reference to the Subject this chapter belongs to. (Relationship: Subject 1:N Chapter)

name.description: The name of the chapter (e.g., 'Kinematics', 'Chemical Bonding').

description.description: A brief description of the chapter content.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Stores the details of a single practice question.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

chapterId.description: Reference to the Chapter this question belongs to. (Relationship: Chapter 1:N Question)

questionText.description: The actual text content of the question.

options.description: An array of possible answer options for the question.

correctOptionIndex.description: The zero-based index of the correct answer option in the 'options' array.

difficultyLevel.description: The perceived difficulty level of the question (e.g., 'Easy', 'Medium', 'Hard').

questionType.description: The category or format of the question (e.g., 'MCQ Single Correct', 'Assertion Reason', 'Numerical Answer').

topicTags.description: An array of keywords or tags for finer topic categorization within a chapter.

UserExamConfiguration.title: UserExamConfiguration
UserExamConfiguration.description: Stores a user's personalized settings and goals for a specific exam.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserExamConfiguration entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this configuration. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserExamConfiguration)

examId.description: Reference to the Exam for which this configuration applies. (Relationship: Exam 1:N UserExamConfiguration)

targetScore.description: The user's desired target score for the selected exam.

dailyStudyHours.description: The number of hours the user commits to studying daily.

examDate.description: The target date for the user to take the exam.

QuestionAttempt.title: QuestionAttempt
QuestionAttempt.description: Records a single attempt by a user on a specific question, capturing performance metrics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuestionAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the attempt. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N QuestionAttempt)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question that was attempted. (Relationship: Question 1:N QuestionAttempt)

examAttemptId.description: Optional reference to the ExamAttempt if this question was part of a larger test. (Relationship: ExamAttempt 1:N QuestionAttempt)

selectedOptionIndex.description: The zero-based index of the option selected by the user.

isCorrect.description: Indicates whether the user's attempt was correct.

timeTakenSeconds.description: The time, in seconds, the user spent on this question.

attemptedAt.description: Timestamp when the question was attempted.

confidenceLevel.description: User's self-reported confidence level when answering (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High').

ExamAttempt.title: ExamAttempt
ExamAttempt.description: Records a user's overall performance in a mock exam or a sectional test.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExamAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who took the exam. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ExamAttempt)

examId.description: Reference to the Exam this attempt is for. (Relationship: Exam 1:N ExamAttempt)

score.description: The total score obtained in this attempt.

totalQuestions.description: The total number of questions in the exam/sectional.

correctAnswers.description: The count of questions answered correctly.

incorrectAnswers.description: The count of questions answered incorrectly.

unattemptedQuestions.description: The count of questions that were not attempted.

timeTakenMinutes.description: The total time, in minutes, spent on the exam/sectional.

attemptedAt.description: Timestamp when the exam/sectional was attempted.

UserChapterPerformance.title: UserChapterPerformance
UserChapterPerformance.description: Aggregates a user's performance and progress metrics for a specific chapter.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserChapterPerformance entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserChapterPerformance)

chapterId.description: Reference to the Chapter. (Relationship: Chapter 1:N UserChapterPerformance)

totalQuestionsAttempted.description: The total number of questions from this chapter attempted by the user.

correctQuestions.description: The count of questions answered correctly from this chapter.

accuracyPercentage.description: The calculated accuracy percentage for this chapter (0-100).

averageTimePerQuestionSeconds.description: The average time spent by the user on questions from this chapter.

lastAttemptedAt.description: Timestamp of the most recent question attempt in this chapter.

chapterCompletionStatus.description: The current completion status of the chapter (e.g., 'Not Started', 'In Progress', 'Completed').

performanceTrend.description: An indicator of recent performance trend (e.g., 'Improving', 'Stable', 'Declining').

RevisionItem.title: RevisionItem
RevisionItem.description: Represents an item (question or chapter) added to a user's revision queue.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RevisionItem entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who needs to revise this item. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N RevisionItem)

questionId.description: Optional reference to the Question if the revision item is a specific question. (Relationship: Question 1:N RevisionItem)

chapterId.description: Optional reference to the Chapter if the revision item is an entire chapter. (Relationship: Chapter 1:N RevisionItem)

itemType.description: Specifies whether the item for revision is a 'Question' or a 'Chapter'.

reasonForRevision.description: The reason why this item was flagged for revision (e.g., 'Low Accuracy', 'Forgotten Topic').

priorityLevel.description: The priority level for revising this item (e.g., 'High', 'Medium', 'Low').

addedAt.description: Timestamp when the item was added to the revision queue.

lastReviewedAt.description: Optional timestamp of the last time this item was reviewed.

UserPsychologicalState.title: UserPsychologicalState
UserPsychologicalState.description: Captures and interprets a user's current psychological state relevant to their study and performance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserPsychologicalState entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserPsychologicalState)

anxietyLevel.description: Current assessment of the user's anxiety level (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High').

focusLevel.description: Current assessment of the user's focus level (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High').

burnoutRisk.description: Assessment of the user's risk of burnout (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High').

confidenceMismatchRisk.description: Assessment of potential mismatch between self-reported confidence and actual performance (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High').

retentionDecayRisk.description: Assessment of the risk of forgetting previously learned material (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High').

reportedAt.description: Timestamp when this psychological state was assessed/reported.

interpretation.description: A human-readable interpretation of the psychological state, generated by AI (e.g., 'Low anxiety, but confidence unstable in Physics').

NextBestAction.title: NextBestAction
NextBestAction.description: Represents an AI-generated recommendation for the user's most impactful next study task.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NextBestAction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile for whom this action is recommended. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N NextBestAction)

actionType.description: The type of action recommended (e.g., 'Revise Chapter', 'Practice Question Type', 'Attempt Sectional Exam').

targetChapterId.description: Optional reference to a Chapter if the action involves a specific chapter.

targetQuestionType.description: Optional string identifying a specific question type if the action involves practicing a type (e.g., 'Assertion Reason').

targetExamId.description: Optional reference to an Exam if the action involves taking a sectional or mock exam.

reason.description: A human-readable explanation of why this action is recommended (e.g., 'low accuracy 42%').

impact.description: A human-readable explanation of the expected improvement from taking this action.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when this action was generated.

isCompleted.description: Indicates if the user has completed this recommended action.

completedAt.description: Optional timestamp when the action was completed.

QuestionTypePerformance.title: QuestionTypePerformance
QuestionTypePerformance.description: Aggregates a user's performance metrics for specific question types across an exam or globally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuestionTypePerformance entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N QuestionTypePerformance)

examId.description: Reference to the Exam this performance data is contextualized to. (Relationship: Exam 1:N QuestionTypePerformance)

questionType.description: The specific type of question (e.g., 'Assertion Reason', 'MCQ Single Correct').

totalAttempted.description: Total number of questions of this type attempted by the user.

correctAttempts.description: Count of correct attempts for this question type.

accuracyPercentage.description: Calculated accuracy percentage for this question type (0-100).

averageTimeSeconds.description: Average time spent by the user on questions of this type.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of the last update to this performance summary.

definition.description: Root document for a user's profile and aggregated dashboard KPIs. Includes denormalized fields like 'totalChaptersCompletedCount', 'totalQuestionsSolvedCount', 'examsTakenCount', 'globalMockAccuracy', 'last7DaysPerformanceTrend', 'weakestChapterSummary', 'weakestQuestionTypeSummary', and 'timeManagementSummary' for efficient dashboard loading.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: User's personalized settings and goals for a specific exam.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: configId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's exam configuration.

definition.description: Records a single attempt by a user on a specific question. Includes denormalized 'questionDifficultyLevel', 'questionType', 'questionChapterId', 'questionSubjectId', and 'questionExamId' for robust querying and filtering.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question attempt.

definition.description: Records a user's overall performance in a mock exam or sectional test.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exam attempt.

definition.description: Aggregated performance data for a user on a specific chapter. Includes denormalized 'chapterName', 'subjectId', and 'examId' for context in queries and UI.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: chapterId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chapter.

definition.description: Aggregated performance metrics for a user on a specific question type, contextualized by exam.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: questionTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier representing the question type (e.g., 'AssertionReason').

definition.description: A user's personalized queue of items (questions or chapters) needing revision. Includes denormalized 'targetChapterName' (if chapter) or 'targetQuestionText' (if question) for direct UI rendering.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: revisionItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the revision item.

definition.description: Historical or current psychological state assessment and AI-generated interpretation for the user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: stateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the psychological state record.

definition.description: AI-generated actionable tasks for the user, including the 'reason' and 'impact' for the recommendation.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: actionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the recommended action.

definition.description: Global collection of competitive exams supported by the platform. Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exam.

definition.description: Global collection of academic subjects. Includes 'examId' for relationship context. Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: subjectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the subject.

definition.description: Global collection of chapters or topics. Includes 'subjectId' for relationship context. Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: chapterId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chapter.

definition.description: Global collection of practice questions. Includes 'chapterId' for relationship context. Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable by adhering to core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation. All user-specific data is organized under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, leveraging path-based ownership. This ensures that security rules are simple and atomic: a user can only access documents where `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls for authorization logic. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
To support the dashboard's `list` operations (QAPs) and prevent `get()` calls in rules, critical data required for display or filtering is denormalized within the relevant documents. For example:
*   The `/users/{userId}` document itself will store aggregated Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) like `totalChaptersCompletedCount`, `totalQuestionsSolvedCount`, `examsTakenCount`, and `globalMockAccuracy`. This allows the main dashboard KPIs to be fetched with a single, highly performant `get()` operation on the user's root document, directly satisfying the user's identity requirement (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Trend indicators and high-level summaries are also stored here, updated by backend processes.
*   `QuestionAttempt` documents will include denormalized fields like `questionDifficultyLevel`, `questionType`, `questionChapterId`, `questionSubjectId`, and `questionExamId`. This allows complex filtering and aggregation for error patterns and time management without requiring security rules to perform cross-collection lookups, making `list` operations on `/users/{userId}/questionAttempts` efficient and secure.
*   `UserChapterPerformance` documents will include `chapterName`, `subjectId`, and `examId` to provide context for 'Weakness Snapshot' and 'Revision Queue' displays without needing to `get()` chapter or subject details separately for each performance record.
*   `RevisionItem` documents will denormalize `targetChapterName` or `targetQuestionText`, along with their IDs, allowing the UI to render revision items directly from a `list` query on `/users/{userId}/revisionItems`, fulfilling the QAP for revision queues.
*   `NextBestAction` and `UserPsychologicalState` entities inherently contain `reason`, `impact`, and `interpretation` fields, which are directly accessible upon fetching the documents from their user-scoped collections.

**Structural Segregation for QAPs:**
*   **Private User Data:** All personalized and sensitive data (attempts, performance, configurations, recommendations, psychological states, revision queues) is strictly segregated under `/users/{userId}/`. This allows for straightforward security rules that grant read/write access only to the authenticated user whose UID matches the path's wildcard.
*   **Global Content Data:** Static content like `Exams`, `Subjects`, `Chapters`, and `Questions` are placed in top-level, separate collections (e.g., `/exams`, `/subjects`). These are typically read-only for all authenticated users, simplifying their security posture and preventing accidental data modification. This segregation ensures that `list` operations on content collections are globally accessible (if desired) without interfering with user-specific data access rules.

This architecture ensures that the dashboard components, from KPIs to dynamic recommendations and weakness snapshots, can be populated efficiently and securely. Rules will primarily check for `request.auth.uid == userId` for private data, and broader read permissions for global content, without complex or performance-hindering `get()` operations for authorization.
